couch.title: Evidence #1 — The Blood Stain
couch.clue:
The blood didn’t spray. It soaked. One dark stain spread across the corner cushion of the couch — deep, concentrated. Someone sat here after they were injured… or they were injured here and never stood back up immediately.

No overturned furniture. No signs of panic.

Just one strange detail: the indentation in the cushion beside the stain. Someone else sat next to them.

Not to help.

To wait.
couch.riddle: I have arms but cannot embrace, and a back but no spine. I wear fabric like skin and hide loose coins in my cushions. What am I?

glass.title: Evidence #2 — Poison In Glass
glass.clue:
A single fingerprint pressed clearly into the side of the glass. Beneath it, a faint streak of blood.

Whoever held this wasn’t bleeding badly — just enough to leave a mark.

The drink inside was untouched.

That’s the unsettling part.

People don’t pour themselves a drink during chaos.

They do it afterward.

Like they knew there wasn’t anywhere else to be.
glass.riddle: I am transparent, fragile, and hold your spirits. If you drop me, I shatter into a thousand sharp, sparkling teeth. What am I?

purse.title: Evidence #3 — Items Within Sight
purse.clue:
The purse looked ordinary until we opened it.

Wallet. Keys. Receipts.

And beneath everything else — a kitchen knife wrapped in a scarf, the blade stained dark red.

Too deliberate to be panic.

Too hidden to be an accident.

Whoever placed it there wanted two things at once:

To keep it close.

And to make sure nobody noticed until they were already gone.
purse.riddle: I go everywhere you go, keeping your life neatly packed in my belly. I am often the first thing taken in a crime. What am I?
